title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. reuteri
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. reuteri, also known as the Reuter's columbine, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Reuter's columbine can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and menstrual cramps.
